pattern_recognition: Matrix Search Problem, specifically a 2D sorted matrix. The rows are sorted and the first element of each row is greater than the last element of the previous row, indicating a structured search space.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check every element in the matrix until the target is found or all elements are exhausted.
approaches.algorithm: Iterate through each row and each column, checking each element against the target.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Utilize the properties of the sorted matrix to reduce the search space efficiently, either by treating the matrix as a single sorted array or using binary search.
approaches.algorithm: Start from the top-right corner of the matrix and eliminate rows or columns based on comparisons with the target.

dry_run: Consider the matrix: [[1, 3, 5], [7, 9, 11], [13, 15, 17]] and target = 9. Starting from the top-right (5), since 9 > 5, move down to (7). Since 9 > 7, move down to (9). Found target = 9.
edge_cases: Empty matrix should return false. Single element matrix should return true if that element is the target. Duplicates should be handled correctly. Large matrices should be tested for performance. Overflow is not a concern with integers in this context.
common_pitfalls: 1) Not considering the sorted nature of the matrix leading to inefficient searching. 2) Failing to handle edge cases like empty matrices or single elements. 3) Incorrectly assuming that all elements are unique, which may lead to unnecessary checks.
